name: Kern
classification_notes: The variable font spans a wide weight and width axis from compressed thin to broader weights, with alternate letterforms for G, R, a and g that suggest a grotesque sans-serif tradition with contemporary flexibility.
summary: A variable grotesque with a wide width axis and alternate letterforms, offered by Pizza Typefaces as a growing family.
description: Kern is a variable sans-serif from Pizza Typefaces spanning weight and width axes, from a compressed thin through to broader cuts. Alternate forms for G, R, a and g add flexibility for display and editorial use, and a soft-set option suggests further stylistic range. The italic exists across widths, making it a versatile grotesque for contemporary graphic work.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Kern as a variable font with axes for size, weight, width and italic, alongside alternate glyph sets including options for G, R, a and g, and a Soft Set mode. The family is described as growing, indicating further styles are in development.

name: Orelo
classification_notes: Orelo presents as a variable grotesque sans-serif with compressed and italic variants, offering a wide range of widths and weights typical of contemporary grotesque type families.
summary: A variable grotesque from Pizza Typefaces with an axis spanning compressed widths and a range of weights from Thin upward.
description: The family operates across multiple variable axes including weight, width, italic and optical size, making it adaptable from tight compressed display settings to looser text configurations. Alternate glyphs for R and a suggest attention to typographic detail and stylistic flexibility. The compressed cuts at thin and light weights give it a particular usefulness in editorial and poster contexts.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Orelo as a variable font with controls for size, weight, width, italic and optical axes, alongside alternate letterforms for select characters. The specimen demonstrates the family across compressed thin and italic cuts using long-form editorial text.

name: Business Blooming
classification_notes: A variable display typeface with optical size axes and alternate soft and ultrasoft uppercase variants, designed for large-scale headline and decorative use rather than continuous text.
summary: A variable display typeface from Pizza Typefaces with optical size control and alternate soft uppercase forms for large-scale setting.
description: Business Blooming offers a variable axis across optical sizes, with alternate soft and ultrasoft uppercase letterforms and an alternate ampersand. The Display subfamily pushes the design toward more expressive, oversized use. Intended for headline and decorative work where letterform softness can be dialled in to suit the context.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Business Blooming as a variable typeface with optical size control, offering alternate soft uppercase, ultrasoft uppercase and ampersand variants, alongside a Blooming Display subfamily optimised for large-scale use.

name: Pleasure
classification_notes: The variable axes — weight, italic, inktrap, and alternates — point to a contemporary grotesque with display ambitions. The inktrap axis and alternate letterforms suggest a modern take on the grotesque genre with expressive optical features.
summary: A variable grotesque from Pizza Typefaces with axes for weight, italic and inktrap depth, plus a suite of alternate letterforms accessible via OpenType features.
description: The family ships as a variable font with at least four axes: weight, italic, inktrap and a randomise function for alternate glyphs. Alternate forms cover a range of characters including A, E, F, S, R and several lowercase letters, giving designers a degree of expressive control within a single file. Two named instances — Standard and Light — anchor the weight range, each with a corresponding italic.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Pleasure as a variable font with adjustable weight, italic, inktrap and random alternate axes, offering multiple stylistic alternates for key characters across the Latin alphabet.

name: Westy
classification_notes: The specimen shows a high-contrast, expressive serif with strong stylistic personality suited to display use. The variable axis controls weight and italic, with a 'Rock' style suggesting a bold, decorative display orientation.
summary: A variable display serif from Pizza Typefaces with axes for weight and italic, offering a range from thin to a bold 'Rock' style.
description: Available as a variable font with weight and italic axes, it spans from a delicate Thin Italic through to a heavier Rock variant with a distinct character. The specimen leans into editorial and poster applications where contrast between weights can be exploited. Pizza Typefaces offer trial fonts alongside purchase, keeping it accessible for testing across sizes.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Westy as a variable font with controls for size, weight, italic and a 'Random' axis, with styles including Rock, Regular, Thin Italic and Light available for trial or purchase.

name: Business Didot
classification_notes: The name and visual specimens confirm a high-contrast Didot-style typeface with the vertical stress, fine hairline serifs and dramatic thick-thin contrast typical of Didone classification.
summary: A variable Didot-style display serif from Pizza Typefaces, offering high contrast and alternates across Light and Book weights.
description: Built around the sharp thick-thin contrast and vertical stress of the Didone tradition, Business Didot is a variable display serif with Light and Book weights alongside italic cuts. The family includes several alternate glyphs for characters such as J, g, f and v/w, giving typographers options for tightening or opening the feel of a composition. A dedicated Display optical size and variable axes for weight, italic and optical size make it adaptable for headline and branding work.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Business Didot as a variable display serif with alternates for key characters and axes covering weight, italic angle and optical size, offered in Light and Book across upright and italic styles.

name: Business Serif
classification_notes: The specimen shows a variable serif with optical size and weight axes, spanning light display cuts to heavier text weights, consistent with a transitional serif designed for editorial range across sizes.
summary: A variable serif from Pizza Typefaces with optical size and weight axes, moving between a light display cut and denser text settings.
description: Business Serif ships as a variable font with at least two primary axes: weight and optical size. The optical size axis shifts the design between a refined display setting at lighter weights and a more robust text configuration at higher optical sizes. The specimen leans into long-form editorial contexts, suggesting a face built to perform across both headline and body copy within a single file.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Business Serif as a variable font with controls for size, weight and optical size, with specimen settings ranging from Display Light to heavier text configurations, demonstrated across extended multilingual editorial copy.

name: Gangsta
classification_notes: The specimen and alternate character options suggest a contemporary grotesque with stylistic alternates and variable weight/italic axes, typical of display-oriented sans-serif grotesque design.
summary: A variable grotesque from Pizza Typefaces with a weight axis running from Thin, an italic axis, and a suite of stylistic alternates for characters including a, g, l, K, k, Q and ampersand.
description: Gangsta is a variable sans-serif with axes for weight and italic, offering a Thin cut in the specimen alongside an italic companion. A set of stylistic alternates covers key characters such as a, g, l, K, k, Q and &, giving designers control over the typeface's personality across a range of display uses. The foundry's playful identity carries through in the naming and specimen copy, positioning it as a characterful but versatile grotesque.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Gangsta as a variable font with weight and italic axes, demoed in a Thin cut alongside a Thin Italic, with multiple stylistic alternate sets for select characters available via toggles in the specimen.

name: Mobilo
classification_notes: Mobilo presents as a playful geometric sans with a variable axis spanning weight and italic, and alternate glyph sets suggesting a friendly, rounded character typical of contemporary geometric sans designs.
summary: A variable geometric sans from Pizza Typefaces with a playful character, offering weight and italic axes alongside a set of alternate glyphs.
description: Built as a variable font, Mobilo spans a weight axis from Thin upward and includes an italic axis, giving designers fine control over expression within a single file. A suite of alternate glyphs — including big uppercases, a wake-up variant, and alternates for 'a', 't', and dotted characters — adds personality without disrupting the underlying geometric structure. The overall tone sits between display friendliness and text utility.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Mobilo as a playful variable font with customisable weight and italic settings, supported by a range of alternate glyph options including alternative uppercases and character variants for added character.

name: ModelStandard
classification_notes: ModelStandard is explicitly presented as a monospaced typeface with variable axes including a monospace axis, italic axis, and weight axis, placing it firmly in the mono category with a neutral sans-serif construction.
summary: A variable monospaced typeface from Pizza Typefaces with axes for weight, italic and a proportional-to-mono blend.
description: ModelStandard is a variable monospaced sans with three axes: weight, italic and a monospace axis that allows the spacing to shift between proportional and full monospaced. Alternate glyphs for R, Q, a and g offer additional character, suggesting the font is aimed at both coding and editorial contexts. The variable approach gives it flexibility that a static monospaced font would not allow.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present ModelStandard as a variable font with axes controlling weight, italic and monospace spacing, with alternate glyphs available for select characters.

name: Bota
classification_notes: Bota appears to be a geometric sans-serif with variable font capabilities, offering a wide weight range from thin to heavy and alternate character options, consistent with a contemporary geometric sans design.
summary: A variable geometric sans from Pizza Typefaces with a wide weight axis and alternate character options for customisation.
description: Offered as a variable font, Bota spans a broad weight range from thin to bold with an italic axis, giving designers continuous control over typographic weight. The family includes alternate glyphs for characters such as r, y, numerals and capital letters, suggesting a considered approach to distinctive detailing. Suited to editorial and display work where typographic flexibility is a priority.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Bota as a variable font with customisable weight and italic axes, featuring a selection of alternate characters including alternate r, y, figures and capitals, giving designers a range of expressive options within a single file.

name: Forno
classification_notes: Forno appears to be a geometric-leaning sans-serif with variable axes including weight, italic, and stylistic alternates, suggesting a contemporary humanist-geometric hybrid approach typical of modern variable type releases.
summary: A variable sans from Pizza Typefaces with axes for weight, italic angle and a waffle optical parameter, plus a suite of stylistic alternates for key letters.
description: Released by Pizza Typefaces, Forno is a variable font with at least four axes: size, weight, italic and a distinctive 'waffle' axis whose effect suggests optical or stylistic modulation. Stylistic alternates are available for lowercase a, g and y alongside alternate numeral and arrow forms, giving designers granular control over the typeface's character. The specimen hints at a range from Thin to heavier weights, with the variable format positioning it for both display and text use.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Forno as a variable font with customisable weight, italic and waffle axes, offering alternate glyphs for select characters and the ability to save custom settings, available in trial and full purchase options.

name: Nuqtra Arabic
classification_notes: A contemporary Arabic variable typeface with upright and italic axes, designed for both Arabic and Persian/Urdu scripts; its clean, geometric construction places it within the Arabic sans category rather than any calligraphic tradition despite the Nastaliq-influenced specimen copy.
summary: A variable Arabic typeface from Pizza Typefaces spanning Hair to Light weights, with an italic axis and support for Arabic, Persian and Urdu scripts.
description: Released as a variable font, it offers a weight axis running from a barely-there Hair to Light, alongside a separate italic axis, giving designers fine control over expression within a single file. The family covers Arabic, Persian and Urdu, with alternate glyph options for several characters including ع, غ, ف, ق and ك. Specimen copy draws on the Nastaliq calligraphic tradition as a cultural reference, though the typeface itself is a contemporary, optically regularised Arabic design.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces presents Nuqtra Arabic as a variable font with weight and italic axes, supporting Arabic, Persian and Urdu scripts, with selectable alternate glyphs for key characters and a specimen setting drawn from texts on Persian and Urdu calligraphic history.

name: Wayle
classification_notes: Wayle appears to be a variable grotesque sans-serif with a weight axis and italic axis, offering stylistic alternates and a range from Thin to heavier weights typical of contemporary grotesque families.
summary: A variable grotesque sans from Pizza Typefaces with weight and italic axes, offering stylistic alternates including alternate R, a, ampersand and slashed zero.
description: A variable grotesque with continuous weight and italic axes, allowing fluid typographic control across a wide range of settings. The family ships with several stylistic alternates, including variant forms for R, lowercase a, the ampersand and a slashed zero, giving designers options to shift the typeface's character. The specimen uses pleasingly absurdist long-form text about pizza history and geological research, which is a reasonable indicator of the foundry's sensibility.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Wayle as a variable font with weight and italic axes, featuring a Thin weight and accompanying italic, with stylistic alternates for select characters including R, a, ampersand, a large Registered symbol and a slashed zero.

name: Hop Expanded
classification_notes: The typeface is an expanded-width sans-serif with variable weight axes and alternate glyph options, placing it squarely in the expanded grotesque category. The interactive controls and alternate forms suggest a contemporary display-oriented grotesque.
summary: A variable expanded grotesque from Pizza Typefaces offering weight and width axes alongside a set of alternate glyphs including G, K, k and y.
description: Available as a variable font, Hop Expanded runs from ExtraThin through to Regular weight with a continuous axis. Alternate glyph options for G, K, k and y give designers additional control over the typeface's character. The expanded proportions and variable nature suit large-scale display settings and editorial headlines.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Hop Expanded as a variable font with adjustable weight and custom axes, featuring alternate letterforms for select characters and a broad expanded stance suited to display use.

name: Hop Compressed
classification_notes: A variable compressed display typeface with extreme vertical proportions and multiple stylistic alternates, squarely in the display sans category with no conventional text application intended.
summary: A variable compressed display sans from Pizza Typefaces offering extreme vertical proportions and several stylistic alternates including single-storey 'a' and alternate 't'.
description: Hop Compressed is a variable display typeface built around a tight compressed axis, running from Thin to Light weights with continuous variation in between. It ships with a set of stylistic alternates — including a single-storey 'a', an alternate 't', and variants labelled Hop and Slim — giving designers control over the character of the face at large sizes. The interactive specimen on the foundry's site lets users toggle alternates and adjust weight in real time, suggesting a typeface aimed at editorial headlines, posters and branding work.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Hop Compressed as a variable font with interactive controls for weight and stylistic alternates, including options for alternate 'a', 't' and custom configurations, available in trial and retail licensing.

name: Shrill
classification_notes: Shrill appears to be a variable grotesque sans-serif with a weight axis and italic axis, featuring alternate letterforms that suggest a contemporary display-leaning grotesque with expressive character options.
summary: A variable grotesque from Pizza Typefaces with a weight axis, italic axis, and a suite of alternate glyphs including alternate G, I, R, K and ligatures.
description: Built as a variable font with at least weight and italic axes, Shrill offers a range of alternate letterforms that give designers control over the typeface's personality. Alternate options for G, g, I, R and K alongside ligature variants for f, t, fi and ffi suggest a typeface intended for display use where fine-tuning character is part of the brief. The specimen leans into expressive, large-scale setting.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Shrill as a variable font with axes for weight and italic, offering alternate glyphs and ligatures for characters including G, I, R, K, f, t, fi and ffi, with a specimen that prioritises large display setting.

name: Campingo Indoor
classification_notes: The specimen text and name suggest a warm, legible serif suited to body text settings at both large and small sizes, consistent with a humanist serif classification designed for editorial or book use.
summary: A text serif from Pizza Typefaces, shown across large and small size specimens with an emphasis on reading comfort.
description: Campingo Indoor is presented in two size settings on the foundry's specimen page, suggesting it is designed with distinct performance at display and text sizes in mind. The specimen copy is set in French and English, indicating at minimum a broad Latin character set. Beyond the name and these specimen details, the page offers little additional information about weights, designer or release year.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Campingo Indoor as a serif typeface with specimen settings at size 30 and size 16, demonstrating its range across headline and body text contexts.

name: Campingo
classification_notes: Campingo is explicitly described as the foundry's Cooper Black font, placing it firmly in the display category as a heavy, rounded display sans with a casual, friendly character.
summary: A rounded, heavy display face in the spirit of Cooper Black, designed for friendly and relaxed typographic work.
description: Designed by Adrien Midzic at Pizza Typefaces, Campingo takes Cooper Black as its reference point and leans into that typeface's warm, bulbous character. Alternate glyphs are available for the lowercase 'a', arrows and slim symbols and punctuation, adding a degree of flexibility for display use. The foundry positions it as the relaxed counterpart to their Indoor typeface.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces describe Campingo as their Cooper Black font, a friendly and 'cluddy' relaxed typeface designed by Adrien Midzic. They contrast it with their Indoor family, which they position as its total opposite.

name: UltraSolar
classification_notes: UltraSolar features unusual letterforms with a distinctive take on inktraps, multiple stylistic sets and dingbats, placing it firmly in the experimental display category rather than any conventional text-type tradition.
summary: An experimental display face by Adrien Midzic, built from a reworking of the Wasa typeface and styled after cosmic and astronomical themes.
description: Designed by Adrien Midzic and produced by Pizza Typefaces, UltraSolar takes the Wasa typeface as a starting point and pushes it into unusual territory with redrawn letterforms, a fresh approach to inktraps and a generous set of stylistic alternates including a Focal set intended for small-size reading. A collection of dingbats accompanies the main character set. The space and star-gazing concept runs through its presentation without overwhelming the practical character of the type itself.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces describe UltraSolar as a new exploration based on the Wasa typeface, inspired by Methuselah, the oldest known star. They highlight its new-era letterforms, unconventional inktraps, weird details, multiple stylistic sets and a set of dingbats, noting that despite its extreme design it remains readable at small sizes, especially through the Focal stylistic set.

name: Version
classification_notes: Version is a geometric sans-serif with a variable weight axis and optional single-storey 'a' and double-storey 'g' alternates, placing it firmly in the geometric sans tradition with contemporary variable font sensibilities.
summary: A variable geometric sans with a weight axis and stylistic alternates for the 'a' and 'g', offered with italic support.
description: Version is a variable-font geometric sans from Pizza Typefaces, adjustable across a weight axis from thin upward with a separate contrast axis. Stylistic alternates include both single-storey and double-storey variants of the 'a' and a double-storey 'g', giving some flexibility in character. The italic cuts match the upright in its variable structure, making it usable across a range of editorial and screen contexts.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Version as a variable font with adjustable weight, contrast and italic axes, alongside alternate letterform options for the 'a' and 'g', with trial fonts available before purchase.

name: Fadoli
classification_notes: Fadoli appears to be a display serif with variable axes including weight and italic, featuring alternate letterforms suggesting an expressive, editorial character suited to large-scale use.
summary: A variable display serif from Pizza Typefaces with weight and italic axes, plus a set of alternate glyphs for key characters.
description: Fadoli is a variable serif designed for display use, offering continuous control over weight and italic angle. A suite of alternate forms for characters including A, K, M, I and their lowercase counterparts gives designers room to adjust the texture and personality of a setting. The Provence label suggests a regional or cultural reference point underpinning its visual character.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Fadoli as a variable font with adjustable weight and italic axes, offering alternate letterforms for select characters and available in trial and full purchase options.

name: Wasa
classification_notes: Wasa presents as a geometric sans-serif with variable weight and italic axes, alternate letterforms for 'a' and 'g', and optional rounded punctuation, placing it firmly in the geometric sans tradition with contemporary variable font sensibility.
summary: A variable geometric sans from Pizza Typefaces with weight and italic axes, offering alternate single-storey 'a' and 'g' and optional rounded punctuation.
description: Available as a variable font spanning at least Thin through Light with a continuous italic axis, Wasa includes stylistic alternates for the lowercase 'a' and 'g' alongside a rounded punctuation option, giving designers control over its personality. The alternate letterforms shift it between a more neutral geometric and a softer, more playful register. Suited to editorial and branding work where a clean geometric base is wanted without fully committing to a single character.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Wasa as a variable font with weight and italic axes, featuring alternate forms for 'a' and 'g' as well as optional rounded punctuation, with trial fonts available alongside purchase.

name: Dozza
classification_notes: The specimen shows a serif with classical proportions, upright stress and varying weights from Book through Regular, with a distinct italic variant labelled 'Regalic', pointing to a transitional or humanist serif lineage with expressive italic forms.
summary: A variable serif from Pizza Typefaces with a weight axis and a distinctly named 'Regalic' italic, suited to editorial and long-form text settings.
description: Dozza is a variable serif offering a continuous weight axis alongside upright and italic cuts, with the italic referred to as 'Regalic' — a detail that suggests the italic was drawn with its own character rather than simply slanted from the roman. The specimen shows the family performing well at both display sizes and extended text, with clean construction across Book and Regular weights. Alternate glyphs for Q and the period add a degree of personality without disrupting the overall composure.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Dozza as a variable font with adjustable weight and italic axes, showcasing named styles including Book and Regular with optional alternate glyphs and a Regalic italic mode.

name: XMX
classification_notes: From the specimen the letterforms appear geometric and monolinear with distinctive alternate characters, suggesting a contemporary geometric sans-serif construction. The name and alternate glyphs for M and 2 hint at a structured, modular design approach.
summary: A geometric sans from Pizza Typefaces with a modular character and notable alternate glyphs for select letters and numerals.
description: XMX is a geometric sans-serif from Pizza Typefaces, distinguished by its structured letterforms and the availability of alternate versions of the M and the numeral 2. The specimen presents it across two size settings, suggesting it works at both display and text scales. Its clean, modular construction suits editorial and branding contexts where a quiet geometric presence is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present XMX as a geometric sans with alternate character options, demonstrated through large display and smaller text specimens that show the typeface's range across different sizes and contexts.

name: Shreck
classification_notes: Shreck is a monospaced typeface offered as a variable font with axes for weight, width and monospace, placing it firmly in the mono category with display-oriented flexibility.
summary: A variable monospaced typeface from Pizza Typefaces with axes spanning weight and width, offering alternate letterforms for key characters via OpenType features.
description: Shreck ships as a variable font with controls for weight, width and monospace, giving designers a single file capable of running from text weights to heavier display settings. Alternate forms for characters such as g, a and R are accessible via OpenType features. The three named instances, Regular, Medium and Bold, anchor the range but the variable axes allow finer steps between them.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Shreck as a variable font with adjustable weight, width and monospace axes, demonstrated across specimen sizes from large display settings down to running text, with optional alternate glyphs selectable through the interface.

name: Metal
classification_notes: Labelled 'Futuristic' on the product page, Metal is a display typeface with variable width and alternates suggesting a sci-fi or techno aesthetic rather than any traditional serif or sans-serif classification.
summary: A variable futuristic display typeface from Pizza Typefaces with Cyrillic support and a range of stylistic alternates.
description: Metal is a variable display face with an adjustable width axis and multiple stylistic alternates, including Cyrillic-specific variants for letters such as A, I, a and the descender forms of Д, Ц and Щ. Its futuristic character makes it suited to editorial headlines, posters and branding where a sci-fi or techno register is wanted. Cyrillic coverage extends its reach beyond Latin-script markets.
foundry_blurb: Pizza Typefaces present Metal as a futuristic variable typeface with a width axis and stylistic alternates, including dedicated Cyrillic alternates for several letterforms, available in trial and full purchase versions.
